    Resources

    Opportunities

    Internships & Research Programs

    • Magee-Womens Research Institute Research Student Training Program (RSTP) — Undergraduate research training program at one of the leading reproductive health research institutes
    • Magee-Womens Research Institute High School Summer Internship Program — Summer research experience for high school students
    • National Institute for Reproductive Health Internships — Policy and advocacy internships focused on reproductive health
    • Stanford Maternal & Child Health Research Institute Drug and Device Development Training Program — Training program at Stanford for drug and device development in maternal-child health

    Professional Societies

    • Society for Women’s Health Research — Advocates for research on biological differences between women and men and promotes women’s health research
    • Society for Maternal-Fetal Medicine — The leading professional society for maternal-fetal medicine specialists; publishes AJOG and hosts an annual meeting

    Career Resources

    • Maternal Health Task Force — Careers in Maternal Health — Profiles of careers in maternal health research, policy, and clinical practice

    Papers & Reviews

    Reviews

    Title Link
    Immunology at the Maternal-Fetal Interface NIH Bookshelf
    Fetal-Maternal Interactions During Pregnancy: A ‘Three-in-One’ Perspective Frontiers in Immunology
    Placental Immunodynamics Regulated by Leukocytes and Trophoblasts: From Pregnancy to Onco-Fetal Immunity ScienceDirect
    Immunological Mechanisms at the Maternal-Fetal Interface Nature Index
    Immunologic Aspects of Preeclampsia Boulanger et al., 2024 — AJOG Global Reports
    Frontiers of Preeclampsia Immune Research PMC

    New Studies

    Title Link
    Gut Microbiota Promotes Tolerance at the Maternal-Fetal Interface Cell
    CCR8+ Decidual Regulatory T Cells Maintain Maternal-Fetal Immune Tolerance During Early Pregnancy Science Immunology
    Human Uterine Natural Killer Cells Regulate Differentiation of Extravillous Trophoblast Early in Pregnancy Cell Stem Cell
    KDM6B-Dependent Epigenetic Programming of Uterine Fibroblasts in Early Pregnancy Regulates Parturition Timing in Mice ScienceDirect
    Feto-Maternal Microchimerism: Memories from Pregnancy Cómitre-Mariano et al., 2022 — iScience
    Immune Cells in Term and Preterm Labor Gomez-Lopez et al., 2014 — Cellular & Molecular Immunology

    Pregnancy Complications

    Title Link
    Preeclampsia: Risk Factors, Diagnosis, Management, and the Cardiovascular Impact on the Offspring Fox et al., 2019 — Journal of Clinical Medicine
    Preeclampsia (StatPearls overview) Karrar et al., 2026 — NIH Bookshelf
    Prevention of Preeclampsia with Aspirin Rolnik et al., 2022 — AJOG
    Pre-eclampsia in Second Pregnancy Campbell et al., 1985 — BJOG
    Fetal and Maternal Contributions to Risk of Pre-eclampsia: Population Based Study Lie et al., 1998 — BMJ
    Recurrent Pregnancy Loss: Causes and Evaluation PMC2709325
    Innate Immunity and Preterm Birth Nature — Cellular & Molecular Immunology

    Maternal-Fetal Immunology

    Title Link
    Immunology of the Maternal-Fetal Interface Erlebacher, 2013 — Annual Review of Immunology
    Role of Immune Cells in Implantation and Pregnancy Maintenance Saito, 2024 — Reproductive Medicine and Biology
    Uterine NK Cells: Active Regulators at the Maternal-Fetal Interface Moffett & Colucci, 2014 — J. Clinical Investigation
